description: Provitamins and vitamins, natural or reproduced by synthesis, incl. concentrates and intermixtures
notes: 5% per the 56th GST Council rate rationalisation (Sep 2025); previously 12%/18% depending on entry.

description: Vitamin A and its derivatives, unmixed
Vitamin B12 and its derivatives, unmixed
Vitamin C (ascorbic acid) and its derivatives, unmixed
Hormones, prostaglandins, thromboxanes and leukotrienes, natural or synthesised; derivatives used primarily as hormones
notes: Includes steroid hormones and insulin as bulk drug substances.

description: Insulin and its salts (bulk)
notes: Formulated insulin for retail sale falls under 300431.

description: Antibiotics (bulk drug substances)
notes: Formulated antibiotic medicaments fall under 300410/300420.

description: Penicillins and their derivatives with a penicillanic acid structure; salts thereof
Streptomycins and their derivatives; salts thereof
Tetracyclines and their derivatives; salts thereof
Chloramphenicol and its derivatives; salts thereof
Erythromycin and its derivatives; salts thereof
Other antibiotics (cephalosporins, quinolones, macrolides, etc.)
Glands and other organs for organo-therapeutic uses; extracts thereof; heparin and its salts
Human/animal blood; antisera, other blood fractions and immunological products; vaccines, toxins, cultures of micro-organisms
notes: Human blood and its components are nil-rated; check the specific 6-digit entry.

description: Antisera and other blood fractions
Immunological products, put up in measured doses or retail packs
notes: Includes monoclonal antibody products.

description: Vaccines for human medicine
notes: Certain notified life-saving vaccines/drugs may be nil-rated by specific notification; verify per product.

description: Other (incl. human blood, cultures of micro-organisms)
notes: Human blood and its components are exempt (nil). Other items under this residual entry may attract 5%; verify per product.

description: Medicaments of two or more constituents mixed together, NOT put up in measured doses or retail packs (bulk/loose)
notes: 5% per the 56th GST Council decision (Sep 2025); previously 12%.

description: Other medicaments, not in measured doses or retail packs
Medicaments put up in measured doses or in forms/packings for retail sale
notes: The core retail-pharmacy heading. 5% per the 56th GST Council decision (Sep 2025), effective 22 Sep 2025; previously 12%. Certain notified life-saving drugs are nil-rated by specific notification.

description: Medicaments containing penicillins/streptomycins or their derivatives, retail packs
Medicaments containing other antibiotics, retail packs
Medicaments containing insulin, retail packs
Medicaments containing corticosteroid hormones, retail packs
Medicaments containing vitamins or products of heading 2936, retail packs
notes: Covers most vitamin/multivitamin formulations sold at the counter.

description: Other medicaments, put up in measured doses or retail packs
notes: Largest category for retail pharmacy items, incl. ORS and most allopathic formulations.

description: Ayurvedic medicaments, retail packs
notes: Ayurvedic/Unani/Siddha/Homoeopathic medicaments follow the medicament rate.

description: Wadding, gauze, bandages and similar articles (dressings, adhesive plasters), impregnated/coated or in retail medical packs
notes: Rate differs from medicaments; verify the current CBIC schedule entry before filing.

description: Adhesive dressings and other articles having an adhesive layer (plasters, adhesive tapes)
Other wadding, gauze, bandages (cotton wool, gauze rolls, crepe bandages)
Pharmaceutical goods specified in Note 4 to Chapter 30 (sutures, reagents, dental cements, first-aid kits, contraceptives)
notes: MIXED-RATE heading: contraceptives (300660) are nil-rated; other sub-headings differ. Prefer the 6-digit entries below.

description: Sterile surgical catgut, suture materials, sterile tissue adhesives, laminaria, absorbable haemostatics
Blood-grouping reagents
Opacifying preparations for X-ray; diagnostic reagents designed to be administered to the patient
Dental cements and other dental fillings; bone reconstruction cements
First-aid boxes and kits
Chemical contraceptive preparations based on hormones or spermicides
notes: Contraceptives are nil-rated (exempt) under the GST exemption schedule. Condoms (4014) are also nil-rated.

description: Gel preparations for use in medical/veterinary procedures (lubricant/coupling gels)
Preparations for oral or dental hygiene (dentifrices, denture powders, mouthwash, dental floss)
notes: Not a medicament: toothpaste/mouthwash attract the standard rate even when sold by a pharmacy. Tooth powder has historically had a lower entry; verify the current CBIC schedule.

description: Dentifrices (toothpaste, tooth powder)
notes: Tooth powder has appeared in a concessional schedule entry in some periods; verify before filing.

description: Yarn used to clean between the teeth (dental floss)
Other oral/dental hygiene preparations (mouthwashes, oral rinses)
Soap; organic surface-active products and preparations for use as soap; skin-wash bars and liquids
notes: Common pharmacy counter item, not a medicament. Rate schedules for daily-use toiletries have been revised by Council decisions; verify the current entry.

description: Toilet soap (bars, cakes) for personal use
Organic surface-active agents; washing and cleaning preparations (detergents, handwash refills)
Instruments and appliances used in medical, surgical, dental or veterinary sciences
notes: Broad device heading (syringes, needles, catheters, diagnostic instruments, thermometers under sub-entries).

description: Syringes, with or without needles
Tubular metal needles and needles for sutures
Catheters, cannulae and the like
Ophthalmic instruments and appliances
Other instruments and appliances (BP monitors, stethoscopes, nebulisers under sub-entries)
notes: Some assistive/diagnostic items have concessional entries; verify per product.

description: Mechano-therapy appliances; massage apparatus; ozone/oxygen/aerosol therapy, artificial respiration apparatus
notes: Oxygen concentrators have had concessional rates during public-health notifications; verify the current entry.

description: Other breathing appliances and gas masks (excl. protective masks without mechanical parts)
Orthopaedic appliances; splints; artificial body parts; appliances worn/carried/implanted to compensate for a defect or disability
notes: Concessional heading: orthopaedic appliances and artificial parts attract 5%; hearing aids (902140) are nil-rated.

description: Orthopaedic or fracture appliances (belts, braces, splints, crutch accessories)
Hearing aids (excluding parts and accessories)
notes: Hearing aids are exempt (nil-rated); parts/accessories attract GST.

description: Artificial joints
Apparatus based on the use of X-rays or of alpha, beta or gamma radiations (medical or otherwise)
notes: 12% for medical/surgical/dental/veterinary use; apparatus for other (industrial) uses attracts 18%. Split heading — classify by end use.

description: Medical, surgical, dental or veterinary furniture (operating tables, examination tables, hospital beds with mechanical fittings; dentists' chairs)
